title: Baseline inventory, test map, and risk report
description: Run build/tests and inspect relevant gesture, hook, overlay, hotkey, diagnostics, settings, and publish areas before code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Produce a short baseline note in the Codex final summary listing exact commands run, current pass/fail status, relevant project structure, risky seams, and proposed execution order; no production behavior changes are made in this issue.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer confirms Codex did not modify production behavior, captured current test/build status, and identified files/tests for later issues.
review_regression_requirements: Run git diff --check and verify only inventory or test-discovery notes changed if any; confirm no app behavior changed.
notes: GC-001 baseline 2026-07-07: branch codex/gesture-optimization created from main to avoid implementing on main. Pre-existing dirty worktree before CSV state update: 20 tracked source/test files modified plus untracked issues/gesture-optimization.csv, GestureExecutionContext.cs, SmartPastePolicy.cs, SmartPasteSourceContractTests.cs, SmartPasteTests.cs; preserve and do not reset. MCP graph ready: project C-Users-Hp-Desktop-loenggg, 5291 nodes, 13938 edges. Commands: python mission_csv_tools.py validate/summarize => VALID rows=13 review_row=yes, all rows open; dotnet build GestureClip.sln --nologo => exit 0, 8 NU1900 warnings because nuget vulnerability index unavailable; dotnet test GestureClip.sln --nologo --verbosity minimal => exit 0, 535 passed, 0 failed; git diff --check => exit 0, line-ending warnings only. Relevant structure: src/GestureClip.Features/Gestures/DirectionGestureRecognizer.cs, MouseGestureService.cs, GestureSettings*.cs, EdgeTriggerService.cs; src/GestureClip.Infrastructure/Gestures/LowLevelMouseHook.cs, Hotkeys/GlobalHotkeyService.cs; src/GestureClip.App/GestureOverlayWindow.xaml(.cs), Services/GestureOverlayService.cs, ClipboardOverlayWindow.xaml(.cs), ViewModels/ClipboardOverlayViewModel.cs, SettingsViewModel.cs, SettingsWindow.xaml; src/GestureClip.Features/Diagnostics/DiagnosticsService.cs; scripts/publish-win-x64.ps1; tests/GestureClip.Tests/Gestures, Hotkeys, Diagnostics, Settings, App. Risky seams: recognizer thresholds and confidence can break intentional gestures; MouseGestureService hook state must not swallow mouse-up/cleanup; overlay path trimming can reintroduce first-last connection; clipboard hotkey toggle must avoid duplicate windows; diagnostics must not expose clipboard contents; SettingsViewModel is large and high-risk; publish/startup artifacts can go stale. Execution order: GC-002 tests, GC-003 recognizer guardrails, GC-004 overlay, GC-005 hotkey toggle, GC-006 settings compactness, GC-007 hook/overlay performance, GC-008 diagnostics, GC-009 regression pack, GC-010 startup/publish, GC-011 smoke fixes, GC-012 phase-2 plan, REVIEW-001 final audit. Initial review: no production behavior modified by GC-001; only CSV state intended. Regression review: build/test/diff-check evidence captured; existing source diffs are pre-existing baseline and not GC-001 behavior changes. Git gate: committing only issues/gesture-optimization.csv baseline state on codex/gesture-optimization; source/test diffs left unstaged.

title: Add anti-jitter and anti-noise gesture recognition tests
description: Create focused tests for direction recognition that cover tiny jitter, random wiggles, short accidental drags, and valid intentional strokes without changing production code first.
acceptance_criteria: Tests exist under tests/GestureClip.Tests/Gestures and fail for at least one missing or weak behavior before implementation; cases include below-threshold movement ignored, zigzag noise simplified, accidental micro-drag rejected, and a valid L/R/U/D stroke recognized.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer verifies tests describe user-visible behavior rather than implementation details and that Codex captured the initial RED failure output.
review_regression_requirements: Run the new gesture tests plus existing gesture test suite; verify failures are only expected RED failures before the matching implementation issue.
notes: Keep this issue test-only unless tiny test helpers are required; do not tune recognizer constants here.

title: Implement stable gesture filtering and mis-trigger guardrails
description: Update gesture recognition/service logic so jitter and accidental乱滑 are filtered while intentional gestures remain responsive.
acceptance_criteria: All tests from GC-002 pass; gesture recognition ignores movement under configured minimum distance, collapses noisy repeated direction samples, rejects gestures with insufficient confidence, and existing valid gesture tests still pass.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer checks implementation is small, configurable where appropriate, and does not hard-code test-only behavior.
review_regression_requirements: Run dotnet test GestureClip.sln with gesture filters; manually smoke-test right-button gesture start, valid stroke trigger, and accidental tiny movement not triggering.
notes: High-risk behavior change; prefer conservative thresholds and clear diagnostics for rejected gestures without spamming logs.

title: Fix gesture trajectory overlay rendering and cleanup behavior
description: Improve overlay path rendering so it does not connect first and last points, disappears smoothly, and keeps long trajectories visually clean.
acceptance_criteria: Automated or viewmodel-level tests verify open polyline behavior and max retained point count; manual test confirms no首尾连线, no abrupt ugly disappearance, and long gestures are clipped or decimated to a readable trail.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer inspects XAML/viewmodel diff for minimal rendering changes and confirms tests cover point retention or path generation.
review_regression_requirements: Run overlay-related tests, full gesture tests, and manual UI smoke test with short and long gestures; capture final summary with observed behavior.
notes: Avoid flashy animation; goal is calm, readable, low-distraction office-tool feedback.

title: Make clipboard overlay hotkey toggle open and close
description: Change clipboard hotkey behavior so pressing the configured hotkey once opens the clipboard overlay and pressing it again closes it.
acceptance_criteria: Hotkey tests prove closed-to-open and open-to-closed toggling; repeated hotkey presses do not create duplicate overlay instances; Escape or existing close path still works if currently supported.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer verifies tests cover the toggle state machine and that implementation does not break existing hotkey registration.
review_regression_requirements: Run tests/GestureClip.Tests/Hotkeys and relevant overlay/viewmodel tests; manually press the clipboard hotkey twice in the running app and confirm visible open then close.
notes: Watch for stale running EXE during manual verification; ensure the tested app build is current.

title: Compact clipboard and gesture settings layout
description: Refine SettingsWindow layout so clipboard and gesture settings use less vertical space while preserving readability and beginner-friendly labels.
acceptance_criteria: Settings window displays clipboard and gesture sections with reduced vertical scrolling at default window size; no control overlaps or clipped text; all existing setting bindings still save and reload; screenshot or manual notes identify checked sections.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer verifies UI changes are scoped to settings compactness and do not remove important beginner-facing explanations.
review_regression_requirements: Run settings tests if present, build the WPF app, open Settings, change at least one clipboard and one gesture option, save, reopen, and confirm persisted values.
notes: Do not waste effort on generic copy polish; focus on fitting key controls and making one-screen operation easier.

title: Reduce mouse hook and overlay high-frequency overhead
description: Optimize mouse hook and overlay update path to reduce allocations, redundant dispatcher work, and noisy per-event logging during high-frequency mouse movement.
acceptance_criteria: Tests or instrumentation show throttling/coalescing behavior where appropriate; debug logs no longer emit one line per mouse event in normal operation; gesture functionality remains intact under rapid movement.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer checks no unsafe hook lifecycle changes were introduced and that any throttling cannot drop gesture end events.
review_regression_requirements: Run gesture tests, hook-related tests if present, and a manual rapid-mouse gesture smoke test while checking logs for reduced noise and no missed final action.
notes: Treat hook code as high risk; never swallow mouse-up or cleanup events just to improve performance.

title: Add privacy-safe gesture and hotkey diagnostics snapshot
description: Extend diagnostics report with gesture enabled, hook status, hotkey status, last gesture, last action, and last error while never exposing clipboard contents.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics tests verify required fields are present; tests verify clipboard item text or sensitive clipboard content is not included; manual diagnostics output can be generated from the app or existing command path.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer verifies diagnostics are user-readable, privacy-safe, and do not log raw clipboard contents or secrets.
review_regression_requirements: Run tests/GestureClip.Tests/Diagnostics and search diagnostics output/log code paths for clipboard content leakage; manually generate a report after one gesture and one hotkey action.
notes: Privacy is mandatory; report metadata and states only, never actual clipboard text/images/files.

title: Create gesture optimization regression test command pack
description: Organize a small repeatable regression pack for gesture recognition, overlay behavior, hotkeys, diagnostics, and settings tests so future Codex runs can verify the optimization area quickly.
acceptance_criteria: A script or documented command set exists and runs the targeted test categories without requiring full manual discovery; command exits nonzero on failures; final summary lists exact commands and expected scope.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer confirms commands are project-native, copy-pasteable from repo root, and do not rely on machine-specific absolute paths unless unavoidable.
review_regression_requirements: Run the new regression pack from C:\Users\Hp\Desktop\loenggg and then run dotnet test GestureClip.sln or the existing full-suite command to ensure it does not hide broader failures.
notes: Keep this as a practical Codex verification pack, not a large new framework.

title: Create or verify one-click startup and refresh publish flow
description: If Start-GestureClip.bat is missing, create a repo-relative one-click launcher; if it exists, verify and refresh it. Then verify publish flow still works after changes.
acceptance_criteria: Start-GestureClip.bat exists at repo root and launches the current built app or builds/publishes it via a repo-relative path; publish-win-x64.ps1 or documented publish command completes or exact blocker is recorded; no hard-coded machine-only shortcut is introduced.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer verifies launcher logic is repo-relative, Windows double-click friendly, and does not depend on a local .lnk or stale absolute path.
review_regression_requirements: Run build, Start-GestureClip.bat smoke test, publish script smoke test when possible, and verify output artifact timestamp/path in final summary.
notes: Current checkout may not contain Start-GestureClip.bat; create it if absent. Important for小白交付 and repo download one-click use.

title: Cross-feature smoke pass for gesture, overlay, clipboard, settings, diagnostics
description: Perform a narrow integration cleanup pass only for issues discovered while combining the optimized gesture, overlay, hotkey, settings, diagnostics, and startup paths.
acceptance_criteria: Any fix is tied to a reproduced integration failure; no broad refactor; targeted tests are added or updated; final summary lists each integration issue found and how it was verified.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer rejects unrelated cleanup and confirms each change has a concrete failing scenario or test.
review_regression_requirements: Run the regression pack from GC-009, full test suite, and manual app smoke covering gesture draw, action trigger, clipboard toggle, settings save, diagnostics report, and startup.
notes: This is a catch-and-fix issue, not permission for redesign; keep diffs small.

title: Design Niuma assistant phase-2 CSV
description: After stability optimization, design the next CSV that turns GestureClip into a faster assistant with quick actions, clipboard AI actions, gesture workflows, presets, and configurable input-action-output chains.
acceptance_criteria: A new proposed CSV or design note exists for phase 2; it covers quick action center, clipboard text actions, gesture workflow chains, preset commands, settings model, privacy boundaries, and verification strategy; no large assistant feature is implemented in this row.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer confirms phase-2 scope is separated from current stability work and does not sneak in unplanned broad implementation.
review_regression_requirements: Confirm current phase-1 regression pack still passes and phase-2 artifact is only planning/design unless explicitly approved later.
notes: Bridge from stable GestureClip to 牛马助手: faster daily actions first, not overbuilt chat app.

title: Final review and release-readiness verification
description: Review the completed GestureClip optimization batch end to end and decide whether it is safe for user testing or release packaging.
acceptance_criteria: All prior issue acceptance criteria are checked; git diff is reviewed; dotnet test or approved full suite passes; targeted regression pack passes; one-click startup or publish path is verified; final report lists remaining risks and exact commands run.
review_initial_requirements: Reviewer performs independent initial review of diffs against the original goals and verifies no requirement was silently skipped.
review_regression_requirements: Reviewer runs regression commands after all fixes, checks for privacy leaks in diagnostics, validates startup artifact freshness, and confirms no state fields remain falsely marked complete without evidence.
notes: Last issue only; do not implement new features here except tiny review fixes with explicit verification.
